title: Andropogon scintillans
taxonomy: Andropogon scintillans is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Panicoideae and the tribe Andropogoneae. At the genus level, it is placed in the genus Andropogon. The species is native to Mexico and is also known as variegated bluestem. It is a perennial grass that grows in a variety of habitats.

The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. intro: Andropogon scintillans, also known as "Bilwilla" or "Red Grama Grass," is a perennial plant species belonging to the Poaceae family, native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its extracts have been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
